meta.title: Tokenization: Where Solana’s Tokenization Push Stands — and Where It’s Going Next

transcript: All right. Hi everybody. Um, thanks for being here. I was thinking back to my first Breakpoint back in 2022. Um, it's so exciting to see how much this conference has grown and, um, really appreciate the folks at Bitwise and of course Solana for having me. So, two of my favorite topics: Tokenization and Solana. Um, by way of introduction, uh, my name is Graham Ferguson. I am the Head of Ecosystem at Securitize. Uh, I work daily with protocols, centralized exchanges, decentralized exchanges, and other distribution venues to figure out the best way of ascribing utility and value to tokenized securities. That's what we focus on at Securitize. It's in our name. It's in our DNA. And, uh, I really believe, um, at Securitize that we have the opportunity to really help institutions come on-chain. I sit firsthand in a role that sees this happening on a daily basis. Uh, and very excited to share some of the latest that we've been working on. Um, so let's start with a definition. You probably have heard of this thing called tokenization. Uh, tokenization is the process of converting financial assets into digital tokens on a blockchain. Now, why is tokenization important? Um, several reasons. I'll give a couple here. Uh, but number one: fast settlement. Right, so in traditional finance, assets typically take, uh, days, oftentimes, to settle. Tokenization speeds up this process dramatically, depending on the asset of course, but, uh, this is one big advantage. Um, number two: broad distribution. Crypto has done a tremendous job of distributing all sorts of different assets to the corners of the earth. This is something that has caught the attention of folks in traditional finance. Uh, and another advantage of tokenization. Number three: programmable compliance. Compliance is baked into the asset itself. Right, so it happens in real-time. It's automatic. It's, uh, programmatic. And number four, perhaps my, uh, my favorite of the advantages is the opportunity to have a brand new design space, uh, for financial products. And so I, I live in New York City. A lot of my friends work in traditional finance. This is something that is very interesting to them. Uh, taking the best of what we know and love in DeFi and merging it with traditional finance. So a bit about Securitize. Uh, to give you just a quick overview, we were founded in 2017 by our founder Carlos Domingo. Over the next several years, we launched several tokenized funds, some of which you may have heard of. Uh, we currently have the largest tokenized asset in BUIDL, the largest tokenized venture fund in BCAP, the largest tokenized private credit fund in ACRED, and the largest tokenized stock. Uh, in total, um, and as of last week, these, these numbers were, are about a week old from when I submitted this deck, but we've tokenized, uh, a total of 3.5 billion in AUM, which is what we have today. Um, so moving forward, we also work with the biggest names in the space. We work with BlackRock, we work with Hamilton Lane, VanEck, Apollo, KKR. Uh, and anecdotally, I mean we have tons of interest on the supply side. Uh, now more than ever are we seeing asset managers, issuers, uh, interested in tokenizing all sorts of assets. It's just incumbent on us to figure out, all right, what is the best way to distribute these? Um, and how do we ascribe utility for these assets on-chain? Tokenization today, just to kind of give, uh, a bit of an overview, uh, and this, these data points were pulled from, uh, our friends over at rwa.xyz. We have 36 billion in on-chain RWAs. That's across various different asset classes. Uh, over half a million holders, 250 individual issuers. And I just pulled a few, uh, clippings from some recent news articles over the last year. Um, as I mentioned, now more than ever we're seeing, uh, tremendous interest from institutions in tokenizing assets and, and, you know, moving money on-chain. Um, so let's, uh, I'll take, I'll give you a minute here to, uh, take a look at Solana versus the world when it comes to RWAs. Um, just take a look at this and sort of see what you observe. I'll direct you to... well, I guess the slide didn't quite line up, but look at the, the middle, uh, the middle stats. Um, because the good in that, if you look at that, approximately 20% of all on-chain RWA holders are on Solana. The less good is that only 3% of all on-chain capital in RWA sits on Solana. So what does this mean? Well, it means that Solana has excelled at what it does best, which is attracting retail, building retail products. We see, um, Backed's X stocks, we see Galaxy's tokenized stock, uh, BUIDL as a backing asset for stablecoins on, on Solana. But what does this mean in terms of opportunity? Right? The opportunity is to bring institutions on-chain. Uh, we need to give institutional participants a reason, a financial incentive, to move capital on-chain. And that's by building products, uh, that do just this, um, that are possible on Solana. So how do we do this? Utility. Uh, that's a gross oversimplification of, of how this works, but, uh, as I mentioned, a large part of my responsibility is figuring out how do we drive utility to these assets on-chain. Um, as I mentioned, investors need a financial incentive to do this, right? And, uh, in my work at Securitize, uh, I have found over the last, um, several months that I, I, this really does seem to be a coordination problem. There's a lot of different moving pieces, a lot of parts that need to come together to make this process work. And I'll explain why. So take the example of RWAs as collateral in DeFi, um, where an individual or an investor will post collateral, uh, in the form of an RWA. They will take out a stablecoin to, um, that, uh, that's been lent against it, and then either post that stablecoin to loop the product or they'll find some stablecoin yield elsewhere. Uh, but in order to build this type of product, you have to coordinate with the lending market. The RWA, the asset issuer has to be comfortable with tokenizing an asset. Uh, an oracle to provide price feeds. Uh, you need a curator to manage risk. You need a stablecoin to serve as that initial pool of liquidity that people can borrow against. You need a blockchain like Solana to participate and be that underlying infrastructure. And you also need KYC and AML protections. So a lot of different moving pieces, right? So with that, excited to share that we did recently go live with a native RWA market on Solana with our friends at Loopscale. Uh, in partnership with Apollo and of course, uh, the great folks at the Solana Foundation. So I'll explain exactly the coordination effort that took place to make this happen. Um, Loopscale was the lending protocol. Apollo is the RWA. Pyth served as our price feed. Um, Paxos USDG, the Global Dollar Network, as that pool of stablecoin liquidity. Uh, and of course, uh, Solana as the underlying infrastructure. And Securitize provided, uh, the KYC and AML services that we do to make this happen. You can see this is a screenshot from the experience on Loopscale. Um, but really, really excited for this. We believe that this is, uh, the first of many products of, of its kind. We're currently iterating on it, taking in, uh, plenty of user feedback from the experience. Um, but, uh, this is a, a giant step forward we believe in offering institutional-grade type products natively on Solana. And so why is this a big deal? Like I said, it's native, right? So ACRED represents, uh, a share directly of the Apollo Diversified Credit Fund, right? So it's not a wrapped token. You're not dealing with some sort of counterparty risk with an entity that you may not know or be able to trust. Apollo is the counterparty. And then it's also custom. Um, I, I have to give a shout out to our, our friends, uh, the founders Mary and Luke at BridgeSplit. They did a wonderful job of working with us to figure out how to reconfigure their protocol to fit the needs of Securitize's obligations as both, as both a registered transfer agent and broker-dealer. So looking ahead, I love slides like this 'cause, well, there's a lot of big numbers and the chart is going up and to the right. Um, but, uh, even the most conservative of these has 2 trillion in, uh, RWAs, uh, tokenized by the year 2030. So if you remember on one of the previous slides, I mentioned we've tokenized in total 36 billion dollars today. Uh, 2 trillion, if my math is correct, is around a 50x, right? And that's conservative. Uh, in crypto we don't yet understand, I believe, uh, exactly how big the market is for tokenization. As I said, you know, there's tons of institutions that are showing interest in these products on a daily basis. Uh, and the market is growing tremendously and we believe that it's going to continue growing exponentially into the future. So with that, uh, please reach out to me if you fall into one of these buckets. We love working with protocols as I said. Um, if you're interested in figuring out how to get RWAs plugged into your flows, if you want to offer RWA markets, I would love to chat with you. Uh, if you're an asset issuer looking to tokenize something, uh, we love creative ideas when it comes to the issuer side of things. Um, if you're a stablecoin, if you're interested in exploring using some of Securitize assets as, uh, the backing for your stablecoin, please reach out. And then, uh, also if you're interested in being the cash leg of some of these RWA trades, uh, there's a tremendous opportunity there to participate and be an innovator in these markets. Um, and then of course if you're an investor as well, interested in learning about how these products work, how we're building institutional-grade products on-chain, um, I would love to hear from you. So with that, um, here's my contact information. Uh, a pleasure to see you all and thank you very much.

enrichment.generated_query: Securitize official announcement blog Solana Breakpoint 2025

tags.impact_reason: Securitize is a market leader in RWAs, and this keynote provides a critical status update on institutional adoption and the $3.5B tokenized asset landscape.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a 'Tokenization Conduit' or high-tech plumbing as the anchor because the standfirst explicitly mentions 'productive plumbing' and the core concept is the conversion of traditional financial assets (private credit) into liquid digital tokens ('yield loops') on Solana. This machine visually represents that process of transformation from solid to liquid. I included the text 'YIELD' on a diegetic digital display because the headline emphasizes the activation of 'yield loops', making the text a critical label for the machine's output in a way that feels native to the high-tech, 'Wired' style environment.
visual_idea.description: In a sleek, dark, server-room environment, a central, complex machine made of polished chrome and dark metal pipes—the Tokenization Conduit—is the focus. A heavy, dark block representing private credit is drawn into an intake, dematerializing into a vibrant, flowing stream of turquoise and magenta liquid light (Solana's colors). This glowing liquid flows through translucent acrylic pipes that recede into the background, forming a complex network. On the side of the main conduit, a small, integrated LED screen glows with the cool white text 'YIELD', indicating the machine's function.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A monumental, hyper-detailed Tokenization Conduit machine stands central in a sleek, dark, server-room environment, composed of highly polished chrome and dark metal pipes, catching sharp, dramatic reflections and deep shadows. A heavy, dark block, symbolizing private credit, is drawn into an intake, visibly dematerializing into a vibrant, flowing stream of mesmerizing turquoise and magenta liquid light, mimicking Solana's colors. This intensely glowing liquid pulses and flows through a complex network of translucent acrylic pipes that gracefully recede and spread into the background, casting dynamic volumetric light within the dark space. On the side of the main conduit, a small, integrated LED screen glows with cool white, deeply illuminated characters forming the text 'YIELD', indicating the machine's critical function. The scene is meticulously illuminated with dramatic, focused volumetric lighting, creating profound depth and strong contrast, emphasizing the machine's epic scale and intricate details, with ample negative space above, suggesting immense height in a vertical 3:4 composition. The polished chrome surfaces exhibit ultra-photorealistic light scattering and reflections, while the translucent conduits subtly refract the internal glowing liquid, contributing to an overall cinematic and high-end atmosphere. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, cinematic lighting, dramatic shadow falloff.

editorial.headline: Securitize and Apollo Activate Native Private Credit Yield Loops on Solana
editorial.standfirst: Institutional capital has historically avoided Solana due to a lack of productive plumbing. A new integration between Securitize, Loopscale, and Paxos changes the calculus, turning private credit into liquid DeFi collateral.
key_takeaways: The Stack: A fully integrated market combining Apollo (Issuer), Loopscale (Lending), Pyth (Oracle), and Paxos USDG (Liquidity).
The Opportunity: While Solana hosts 20% of all RWA holders, it holds only 3% of on-chain capital—this product directly targets that valuation gap.
Native Utility: ACRED tokens are no longer static; they can be used as direct collateral to borrow USDG, enabling on-chain leverage for private credit.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
For years, the narrative around Real World Assets (RWAs) on Solana has been one of volume without value. According to **Securitize** Head of Ecosystem Graham Ferguson, the network boasts 20% of all on-chain RWA holders—driven by retail adoption—but captures a meager 3% of the total capital. The barrier hasn't been interest; it has been utility. Institutional investors don't just want to hold a tokenized receipt; they want to put that capital to work. The newly announced integration between Securitize, **Apollo**, and **Loopscale** marks the first serious attempt to close this gap by turning private credit into productive DeFi collateral.

This is not a simple tokenization pilot. Ferguson detailed a complex "coordination problem" solved by aligning four critical infrastructure layers: **Apollo** as the issuer of the ACRED token, **Loopscale** as the lending protocol, **Pyth** for real-time price feeds, and **Paxos' USDG** (Global Dollar Network) as the liquidity layer. The result is a native market where investors can post shares of the Apollo Diversified Credit Fund as collateral to borrow stablecoins. This effectively creates an on-chain leverage loop for private credit, a strategy previously restricted to opaque off-chain prime brokerage desks.

Crucially, this integration eschews "wrapped" assets in favor of native issuance with programmable compliance. Ferguson highlighted the work of **BridgeSplit** in reconfiguring the underlying protocol to satisfy Securitize’s requirements as a registered transfer agent and broker-dealer. This ensures that KYC and AML checks are not a bottleneck but a baked-in property of the asset itself, executing automatically during the lending process. This "programmable compliance" allows institutions to interact with DeFi protocols without breaching regulatory perimeters.

With Securitize currently managing $3.5 billion in tokenized AUM and conservative estimates projecting the RWA market to hit $2 trillion by 2030, the race is on to own the "cash leg" of these trades. For VCs and developers, the alpha lies in the infrastructure that supports these flows. The Loopscale integration proves that high-value assets can live natively on Solana, provided the plumbing exists to make them liquid. The era of static tokenization is ending; the era of institutional DeFi utility has begun.
